name: Boston Scientific Corporation

profile.description: Boston Scientific develops medical devices across cardiology, endoscopy, urology, and neuromodulation. FY2025 revenue of $20.1B (+20% YoY) with Q1 2026 at $5.2B (+11.6%). FY2026 guidance: 7-8.5% growth, EPS $3.34-3.41. Headquartered in Marlborough, Massachusetts.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Boston Scientific grew FY2025 revenue 20% to $20.1B, driven by successful product launches across cardiology (FARAPULSE PFA), endoscopy, and neuromodulation, representing strong multi-category innovation [1]. AI Deployment (1/2): BSX integrates AI in imaging analysis and procedural guidance, but no specific AI revenue quantified. Market Share (2/2): Category leader in electrophysiology (FARAPULSE), peripheral interventions, and endoscopy with meaningful share gains across all three divisions. Product Pipeline (2/2): FARAPULSE expansion, WaveWriter Alpha spinal cord stimulation, and next-gen structural heart devices provide multi-year product cycle visibility. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D at ~10% of revenue is at industry average.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): BSX's mission of 'advancing science for life' connects medical device innovation to patient outcomes — genuine but standard for the industry. Community Impact (1/2): Medical devices directly improve patient health outcomes. Employee Retention (1/2): Generally stable medtech workforce with specialized engineering talent. Insider Buying (0/2): No notable insider buying identified.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. Q1 2026 revenue of $5.2B grew 11.6% YoY with adjusted EPS of $0.80 (+6%) [1]. Forward P/E of 12.6 is below the 20 target. Profit margin of 17.3% is near the 20% target. ROE of 14.7% is near the 15% target. D/E of 42.3% is well below the 70% target. Revenue growth of 11.6% exceeds the 10% target. FY2026 guidance of 7-8.5% growth with EPS of $3.34-3.41 was lowered from initial expectations due to urology headwinds and margin compression [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Medical device manufacturing, FDA regulatory approval, and physician adoption cycles create deep structural moats that AI cannot substitute [1]. Physical devices — stents, pacemakers, spinal cord stimulators — require precision manufacturing and clinical validation. AI may help competitors design better devices faster, but BSX's installed base and physician relationships provide durable protection. AI Opportunity (1/1): BSX deploys AI in imaging analysis, procedural guidance, and device optimization, enhancing product value and physician outcomes.

infinite_game.summary: Boston Scientific has a strong innovation culture built over 45+ years but operates primarily as a financial optimization engine, acquiring and integrating medical device companies for growth rather than pursuing a transformative purpose.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): 'Advancing science for life' is genuine but standard for medtech. Strategic decisions are driven by category leadership and margin optimization rather than a distinctive purpose [1].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): Stable medtech culture with specialized engineering talent. Generally positive employee sentiment.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): BSX frames competition with Medtronic and Abbott as market share battles.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): BSX has evolved from a stent company to a diversified medtech platform through strategic acquisitions (BTG, Baylis Medical, FARAPULSE) [1].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Heavy investment in FARAPULSE pulsed field ablation technology, betting on a new electrophysiology paradigm.

investment_thesis: Boston Scientific offers medtech leadership at 12.6x forward earnings with FY2025 revenue growing 20% to $20.1B and Q1 2026 at $5.2B (+11.6%) [1]. The FARAPULSE electrophysiology platform, spinal cord stimulation expansion, and endoscopy innovation drive multi-category growth. D/E of 42% and adjusted EPS growth of 9-11% (FY2026 guidance) provide financial stability. The $70B market cap is supported by consistent mid-teens organic growth and margin expansion potential.
thesis_continued: The primary concern is decelerating growth from the 20% FY2025 pace to 7-8.5% FY2026 guidance, with management lowering near-term expectations due to urology headwinds and margin compression [1]. Profit margin of 17.3% and ROE of 14.7% are both just below their respective targets. The medtech sector faces pricing pressure from hospital purchasing organizations and potential tariff exposure on overseas manufacturing. Competition from Medtronic's and Abbott's AI-enabled devices could erode BSX's innovation premium over time.
key_catalysts: FARAPULSE pulsed field ablation platform expansion driving electrophysiology category leadership with double-digit growth trajectory [1]
FY2026 EPS guidance of $3.34-3.41 (+9-11%) with margin expansion potential as product mix shifts toward higher-margin categories
Pipeline launches in structural heart, neuromodulation, and endoscopy providing multi-year product cycle support
key_risks: Growth deceleration from 20% (FY2025) to 7-8.5% (FY2026 guidance) raises questions about sustainability of the premium growth narrative [1]
Urology segment headwinds and margin compression flagged by management could worsen if competitive pressures intensify
Hospital purchasing consolidation and potential tariff exposure on medical device manufacturing could compress margins
sources: Boston Scientific Q1 2026 Earnings and FY2026 Guidance — Yahoo Finance, Simply Wall St, TipRanks, 2026
Yahoo Finance — BSX Key Statistics and Financial Data, accessed June 2026
